title: Context, Cancellation, Backpressure, Structured Concurrency, and Leaks · Goroutine Forensics
summary: A request launches several operations; one fails, one blocks, one outlives the caller, and overload grows without bound. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a cancelable bounded worker group and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pass context as the first operation parameter across call boundaries and avoid storing it as durable domain state.
Derive, propagate, call, and observe cancellation, deadlines, timeouts, and causes while releasing timer resources.
Use context values only for request-scoped cross-boundary metadata with collision-safe keys, not optional parameters.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Context Propagation
steps.instruction:
debug case — Request 5 launches validation, lookup, and audit work under a 100ms deadline. One stage blocks after cancellation and the input queue grows. Bound concurrency, propagate cause, choose overload behavior, and prove no goroutine remains.

Inspect the Context Propagation trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Context Propagation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for pass context as the first operation parameter across call boundaries and avoid storing it as durable domain state. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Context Propagation, keep Context Propagation connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: For Context Propagation, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Context Propagation, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A Context belongs in every struct so future methods can reuse it.
